info.title: Ad Studio API
info.description: Backend API for Ad Studio - AI-powered video advertisement creation

post.description:
Authenticate or register a user via Google Sign-In.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/auth/auth_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • User taps "Continue with Google" on AuthScreen
  • After Google Sign-In SDK returns an id_token on the device

FLOW:
  1. Flutter calls google_sign_in package → receives id_token
  2. POST that token here
  3. API upserts user (creates if new, fetches if returning)
  4. Returns JWT access_token + full user object

STORE THE RESPONSE:
  • Save access_token in flutter_secure_storage
  • Save user object in your auth provider / Riverpod state
  • Attach to all future requests:
        dio.options.headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer $token';

NAVIGATE AFTER SUCCESS:
  • First-time user  → OnboardingScreen (lib/features/onboarding/)
  • Returning user   → MainNavigationScreen (lib/features/main_navigation/)

REQUEST:  { "id_token": "<google_id_token>" }
RESPONSE: { "access_token": "eyJ...", "token_type": "bearer",
            "user": { id, name, email, avatar, googleId, createdAt } }

NOTE: Currently mock auth for development. Wire up real Google token
verification before release.

Register a new user with explicit credentials (non-Google).

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/auth/auth_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • Fallback if Google Sign-In fails and you add email registration
  • Currently AuthScreen only shows Google Sign-In, so this is
    a developer / admin endpoint for manual user creation

NAVIGATE AFTER SUCCESS: same as /google → OnboardingScreen or HomeScreen

REQUEST:
  { "email": "user@example.com", "name": "Jane",
    "google_id": "uid_string", "avatar": "https://..." }

⚠️  DEVELOPMENT ONLY — bypasses Google OAuth for Swagger testing.

Only works when the DEV_MODE=true environment variable is set on the server.
This endpoint is automatically disabled in production (DEV_MODE defaults to false).

HOW TO USE IN SWAGGER:
  1. Call this endpoint with any email (e.g. test@example.com)
  2. Copy the access_token from the response
  3. Click the 🔒 Authorize button at the top of /docs
  4. Enter:  Bearer <paste_token_here>
  5. All protected endpoints will now work

REQUEST:  { "email": "test@example.com", "name": "Test User" }
RESPONSE: { "access_token": "eyJ...", "token_type": "bearer", "user": {...} }

get.description:
Get the authenticated user's own profile record.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/profile/profile_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • ProfileScreen.initState / didChangeDependencies to populate header
    (name, avatar, email, memberSince)
  • PREFER: use GET /api/app-data which returns user + stats in one call

REQUEST:  Authorization: Bearer <token>
RESPONSE: { id, name, email, avatar, googleId, createdAt }

put.description:
Update the authenticated user's name and/or avatar URL.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/profile/profile_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • User taps the edit / pencil icon on ProfileScreen and saves changes
  • If uploading a new avatar image: upload to Cloudinary first via
    POST /api/brands/with-logo or a dedicated upload endpoint, then
    send the returned URL in the `avatar` field here

FIELDS (all optional, only include what changed):
  { "name": "New Name", "avatar": "https://res.cloudinary.com/..." }

RESPONSE: full updated UserResponse object

delete.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and all their data.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/profile/profile_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • User taps "Delete Account" button and confirms the danger dialog
    on ProfileScreen

CASCADE DELETION (automatic):
  1. All products belonging to the user's brands are deleted
  2. All brands owned by the user are deleted
  3. The user document itself is deleted

AFTER CALL:
  • Clear flutter_secure_storage (remove access_token)
  • Navigate back to AuthScreen (lib/features/auth/auth_screen.dart)
  • Show a "Account deleted" snackbar/toast

RESPONSE: { "message": "User account deleted successfully" }

get.description:
Get any user's public profile by their ID.

━━━  FRONTEND: Not yet used directly in any screen  ━━━

FUTURE USE:
  • If a shared / collaborative feature is added (e.g., viewing
    another creator's profile), call this endpoint.
  • Currently all user data is fetched via GET /api/users/me or
    the bulk GET /api/app-data endpoint.

REQUEST:  Authorization: Bearer <token>  (no body)
RESPONSE: UserResponse object

post.description:
Create a new brand (JSON body, no file upload).

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/home/widgets/brand_creation_popup.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • Fallback variant if the logo image is already hosted and you have
    a URL ready to pass as the `logo` string field.
  • PREFER: POST /api/brands/with-logo (multipart) when the user picks
    a logo from their gallery inside BrandCreationPopup.

FIELDS:
  { "name": "Acme Co",          // required
    "logo": "https://...",      // optional, pre-hosted URL
    "target_audience": "...",   // optional
    "category": "Fashion" }     // optional

RESPONSE: full BrandResponse including generated brand ID

get.description:
List all brands owned by the authenticated user.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/home/home_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • HomeScreen on initial load to populate the brand card grid.
  • PREFER: GET /api/app-data (returns brands + products + stats in one
    request, minimising round-trips on app startup).
  • Use this standalone endpoint only when refreshing the brand list
    without needing product or stats data.

  Also used by:
    lib/features/search/search_screen.dart – to populate full-text
    search candidates across brand names.

QUERY PARAMS: ?skip=0&limit=100
RESPONSE: BrandResponse[]

schema.title: Response Get Brands Api Brands Get

get.description:
Fetch a single brand's details by its ID.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/brand/brand_detail_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • BrandDetailScreen on mount to display the brand header
    (logo, name, category, targetAudience).
  • PREFER: GET /api/app-data which already embeds each brand object
    with its product list – avoids an extra round-trip.
  • Use this standalone endpoint only when you need an up-to-date
    snapshot of a single brand after an edit/update.

RESPONSE: BrandResponse (single object)

put.description:
Full replacement update of a brand (all fields must be supplied).

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/brand/brand_detail_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • Called from the edit icon / pencil button on BrandDetailScreen
    when you want to send the complete brand body.
  • PREFER: PATCH /api/brands/{id} for partial updates (only changed
    fields) – avoids accidentally blanking optional fields.

BODY: full BrandUpdate object
  { "name": "...", "logo": "...", "target_audience": "...",
    "category": "..." }

RESPONSE: updated BrandResponse

delete.description:
Delete a brand and cascade-delete all its products.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/brand/brand_detail_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • User taps the delete / trash icon on BrandDetailScreen and
    confirms the warning dialog.

CASCADE DELETION (automatic):
  1. All products where brandId == brand_id are hard-deleted
  2. The brand document itself is deleted

AFTER SUCCESS:
  • Navigate back to HomeScreen
  • Remove the brand card from the local brands list
    (or re-fetch GET /api/app-data to refresh full state)

RESPONSE: { "message": "Brand deleted successfully" }

get.description:
Return the product count for a specific brand.

━━━  FRONTEND: lib/features/brand/brand_detail_screen.dart  ━━━

WHEN TO CALL:
  • Lightweight call to refresh the product badge counter on
    BrandDetailScreen without fetching full product objects.
  • PREFER: GET /api/app-data which includes product_count inside
    each BrandResponse – avoids an extra network call.

RESPONSE: { "brand_id": "...", "product_count": 7 }

post.description: Creates a new VideoJob for a product and generates scene/shot templates.

Approves the generated scenes for a VideoJob and moves it to 'processing'.
Triggers background video generation using Kling 3.0 via Kie AI.
Creates a new PosterJob for a product and triggers Nano Banana Pro for an ultra-high-quality poster generation.
